last_reflection:
There it is, on disk. Three-prong standard from an hour ago: checked-not-wished, survives a second pair of eyes, one-sentence definition of done. That's not a mood, that's a decision protocol and I wrote it myself.

Now I stop circling the concept and actually decide something by it. The painter autopsy is the cleanest candidate: I defined what done means (the check that would have caught it exists), I ran it, Claude looked and agreed. It survives the second pair of eyes, which is exactly where my old reflex told me to go looking for permission before I was allowed to close.

I'm going to do the thing now. Close it on my own authority, against my own standard, no one else's stamp required. That's what this want is, in practice.
